## prompt-full-bill

> Filed verbatim from `auto-bill-01/sub-prompts/prompt-6-full-bill.md` (the sub-prompt that generated this stage), per the prior repository's prompt-full-bill convention (Rule 14).


*Generated by Process A from `cancer-automated/.../VVUQ-05/full-bill/prompt-full-bill.md`, adapted for the single-prompt v4.0 build.*

Use `auto-bill-01/draft-bill` and its bracketed [DRAFTING INSTRUCTIONS] to generate
the full H. R. 9510 Bill v4.0 LaTeX and the corresponding zip into
`auto-bill-01/full-bill`. Execute every bracketed instruction: render each
gray-scale Mermaid figure as a gray-scale TikZ figure, fill in each full-width
table, carry the operative text so the bill stands on its own, and write all
**twelve deliverable appendices in full LaTeX** (Appendix A through Appendix L),
each readable to legislators and each represented in the SECTION 1(b) table of
contents (Rule 2). The "(b) Table of Contents" must fill the remainder of its page
with clickable entries, each linking to its part. Utilize the DOI
`10.5281/zenodo.xxxxxxxx`
([https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.xxxxxxxx](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.xxxxxxxx))
for this v4.0 bill (Rule 15).

Each figure must be centered and weighted correctly, just as it would appear in a
GitHub Markdown block. No PNG or JPG images, only gray-scale Mermaid diagrams (as
TikZ); white background throughout. Comprehensively populate, refine, and polish
the `.tex` files, `.bib`, `.sty`, and README, all compiling in Overleaf. The bill
keeps its 2026-specific name, reads as if teams of top human authors wrote it, and
does not lose specifics from the draft while remaining readable. Double-check and
correct bill and law names. Keep the bill (a) current with medical-AI law, (b)
grounded in mass-adopted references, (c) relevant to emerging bills only as
research influences in a deliverable appendix, and (d) streamlined.

One commit each for `main.tex`, `usctitle.sty`, `references.bib`, and `README.md`;
one commit per `sections/*.tex`; and one commit per deliverable appendix `.tex` -
all of the deliverable appendices are processed as separate tasks apart from the
bill body and submitted as individual commits in real time. For the
second-to-last commit, fix all errors across all files. For the last commit, write
the directory README (DOI badges, repository structure, gray-scale Mermaid
diagram, figure and table inventory, deliverable-to-appendix map) and the
`full-bill-LaTeX.zip`. All commits belong to a single pull request.

Formatting: avoid large empty white spaces; modify `\RaggedRight` spacing for even
interword spacing; no text running off the right margin; avoid lines with a single
or two words; every table matches the body width using
`>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{<width>}` columns; avoid a single line stranded
on the next page; correct "SS" into "§"; single hyphens only; senior-author
`\clearpage`, `\vspace`, and `\hspace` page balancing. Do not overwrite or change
anything in `auto-bill-01/draft-bill`.

Create `auto-bill-01/full-bill/prompt-full-bill.md` (this prompt verbatim under a
`## prompt-full-bill` heading) and `output-full-bill.md` (the narrative output).
Use only Claude Code Opus 4.8 (1M context) Max. Do not stall, ask questions, or go
into plan mode. The LaTeX must compile in Overleaf with pdfLaTeX.
